issues.title: Move GPOs out of category "Windows Components"
issues.suggested_reply: @yeelam-gordon — community PR #337 touches `policies/IntelligentTerminal.admx`, `policies/en-US/IntelligentTerminal.adml` (GPO policy metadata; you have recent commits in this area). 7-day SLA clock starts today. Worth a first pass?

issues.title: Eternal Terminal (step 1): per-tab /save-tab + /restore-tab (layout + content)
issues.suggested_reply: Hi @DDKinger, CI is failing on `check-spelling` — quick scan suggests it might be unrelated to your changes, but could you take a look at https://github.com/microsoft/intelligent-terminal/runs/85550128776? If it's flaky, push an empty commit (`git commit --allow-empty -m "ci: rerun"`) to re-run.

issues.title: Make agent bars follow the tab theme color (#348)
Setting delegateAgent to a different agent than acpAgent crashes master process (wta-master pipe connect failure)
issues.suggested_reply:
Sorry you hit a crash @Aust-aa-36. For crashes specifically we need the backend diagnostic data, which you can capture via Feedback Hub: https://aka.ms/intelligent-terminal-feedback. Pick the matching category, hit "Share My Feedback" after submitting, and paste the link here.

I can see the launch failure is the master pipe connection path: <!-- from body -->The system cannot find the file specified. (os error 2)<!-- /from body -->. That plus the Feedback Hub stack should help us pinpoint where wta-master fails during startup.

PS: If you can also attach a zip of `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` after reproducing, that would help correlate the WTA startup failure.

issues.title: Improve Copilot auth flow after install
Stucked on loading
issues.suggested_reply:
Hi @MartinSusky, could you grab a WTA log next time you hit this?

1. Reproduce the issue (select Claude and wait until the agent sessions pane stays on Loading).
2. Zip `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` for unpackaged dev builds).
3. Attach the zip here.

If the issue is hard to reproduce, set `WTA_LOG=debug` first to get the noisier traces.

issues.title: Agent Pane do not render markdown correctly
issues.suggested_reply:
Hi @haonanttt, could you share which **Intelligent Terminal version** you're on? You can find it in the **About** dialog (menu "V" button → About at the bottom). The **Windows build number** is also helpful — run `ver` or `[Environment]::OSVersion` in a shell.

This helps us tell whether it's already fixed in a newer build.

PS: The screenshot shows the Copilot pane rendering literal Markdown markers like <!-- from image -->`**GitHub Copilot CLI**`<!-- /from image -->, so I’d keep this in Area-AgentPane/Agent-Copilot while we wait for the build info.

issues.title: Copilot commands lack OS/Shell environment context (suggests non-existent/non-coreutils commands in Windows Terminal + PowerShell)
issues.suggested_reply:
Hi @indcoder, could you grab a WTA log next time you hit this?

1. Reproduce the issue.
2. Zip `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\*IntelligentTerminal*\LocalCache\Local\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` (or `%LOCALAPPDATA%\IntelligentTerminal\logs\` for unpackaged dev builds).
3. Attach the zip here.

If the issue is hard to reproduce, set `WTA_LOG=debug` first to get the noisier traces. <!-- from image -->The screenshot shows Copilot suggesting `rg -n "TargetPort|planner-hosted|Foundry" AppHost`.<!-- /from image --> That concrete command is helpful; the log should show the shell/context we sent into the Copilot turn so we can fix the prompt path rather than guessing.
